metadata.description: 原型链: JavaScript Inheritance Mechanism. `__proto__` points to the prototype. Property lookup traverses up the prototype chain. Here's a brief explanation with a Python code example to illustrate: In JavaScript, objects inherit properties and methods from their prototypes through a prototype chain. The `__proto__` property allows you to access an object's prototype directly. ```python # Example in Python (Note: Python uses a different inheritance model but can still demonstrate the concept) class Animal: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def sound(self): return "Some sound" class Dog(Animal): def __init__(self, name): super().__init__(name) # Python uses `super()` to call the parent class constructor def sound(self): return "Woof!" # Creating an instance of Dog dog = Dog("Buddy") print(dog.name) # Output: Buddy print(dog.sound()) # Output: Woof! # In JavaScript, you could do something similar with prototypes: class Animal: pass class Dog(Animal): pass Dog.prototype = Object.create(Animal.prototype) Dog.prototype.constructor = Dog dog = new Dog() ``` In the Python example, `Dog` inherits from `Animal`, and we can see how methods are passed down through inheritance. While Python uses a different mechanism (new-style classes with `super()`), it still demonstrates the concept of method overriding and property inheritance.

data_pointers.title: 原型链
data_pointers.content_preview: 原型链: JavaScript Inheritance Mechanism. `__proto__` points to the prototype. Property lookup traverses up the prototype chain. Here's a brief explanation with a Python code example to illustrate: In JavaScript, objects inherit properties and methods from their prototypes through a prototype chain. The `__proto__` property allows you to access an object's prototype directly. ```python # Example in Python (Note: Python uses a different inheritance model but can still demonstrate the concept) class Animal: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def sound(self): return "Some sound" class Dog(Animal): def __init__(self, name): super().__init__(name) # Python uses `super()` to call the parent class constructor def sound(self): return "Woof!" # Creating an instance of Dog dog = Dog("Buddy") print(dog.name) # Output: Buddy print(dog.sound()) # Output: Woof! # In JavaScript, you could do something similar with prototypes: class Animal: pass class Dog(Animal): pass Dog.prototype = Object.create(Animal.prototype) Dog.prototype.constructor = Dog dog = new Dog() ``` In the Python example, `Dog` inherits from `Animal`, and we can see how methods are passed down through inheritance. common_pitfalls: 忘记设置 constructor 属性：在将子类的 prototype 设置为父类实例或 Object.create 后，子类的 constructor 会丢失，导致 instanceof 和类型判断出错，应手动赋值 Child.prototype.constructor = Child。
直接使用 new Parent() 设置原型：使用 Child.prototype = new Parent() 会调用父类构造函数，可能带来不必要的副作用（如设置实例属性），应优先使用 Object.create(Parent.prototype) 来避免。
在原型上存储可变对象：在原型上定义数组或对象属性时，所有实例共享同一引用，修改一个实例会影响其他实例，应避免在原型上放置可变数据，或在构造函数中初始化。
